staticTable.caption: RD maturity values at 7% — for ₹5,000 monthly deposit

examples.heading: Goal-based RD — saving for a vacation in 2 years
examples.text: To accumulate ₹1.3 lakhs for a vacation in 2 years, start a ₹5,000/month RD at 7% interest. After 24 months you will have deposited ₹1.2 lakhs and earned approximately ₹9,264 in interest, giving a maturity value of ₹1.29 lakhs.

examples.heading: Child education fund — ₹10,000/month for 5 years
examples.text: A ₹10,000/month RD at 7% for 5 years accumulates ₹7.26 lakhs on a total investment of ₹6 lakhs. The ₹1.26 lakh interest earned is taxable. For longer horizons, a SIP in a balanced fund may provide better post-tax returns.

examples.heading: RD vs SIP for short-term goals (3 years)
examples.text: For goals under 3 years, RD provides safety and guaranteed returns. SIP in equity funds is volatile over 3 years.

decisionGuide.question: When should you choose RD over SIP?
decisionGuide.answer: Choose RD over SIP when your goal is within 1–3 years and you cannot afford any risk. RDs provide guaranteed returns. For goals beyond 5 years, a SIP in equity mutual funds almost always generates higher post-tax returns despite short-term volatility.

decisionGuide.question: Is RD interest taxable?
decisionGuide.answer: Yes. RD interest is fully taxable as 'Income from Other Sources' at your applicable income tax slab rate. Banks deduct TDS at 10% if total interest income (FD + RD combined) exceeds ₹40,000 in a financial year. Submit Form 15G/15H if your income is below the taxable limit.

decisionGuide.question: Can you withdraw RD before maturity?
decisionGuide.answer: Most banks allow premature RD closure with a penalty of 0.5–1% on the applicable rate for the period held. Some banks charge a flat penalty. Missing monthly RD installments typically attracts a small fine (₹1.5–2 per ₹100 per month of delay).

faq.q: What is the current RD interest rate in India in 2026?
faq.a: RD interest rates in India in 2026 range from 6.5% to 7.5% at major public and private sector banks. SBI offers 6.5–7.0% depending on tenure. Small finance banks offer higher rates up to 8.5%. Senior citizens receive 0.25–0.5% additional interest.

Indian bank RDs use quarterly compounding with a specific formula: M = R × [(1+r)^n − 1] / [1−(1+r)^(−1/3)], where R is monthly deposit, r is quarterly rate, and n is total quarters. This differs from SIP formula as each installment earns interest for a different duration.

faq.q: What is the minimum amount for an RD?
faq.a: Most banks allow RDs starting from ₹100/month. Post offices accept ₹100/month as the minimum. There is no maximum limit on RD deposits, though tax rules apply to interest income above ₹40,000/year.

faq.q: What is the difference between an FD and an RD?
faq.a: An FD requires a one-time lump sum investment. An RD allows you to deposit a fixed amount every month, making it suitable for regular savers. FD is better for investing a large sum at once; RD is better for building savings from monthly income.

faq.q: Can you take a loan against an RD?
faq.a: Yes. Most banks allow loans up to 90% of the RD balance at any point. The loan interest rate is typically 1–2% above the RD rate. This is useful if you need funds urgently without breaking the RD and losing interest.

faq.q: Is RD better than a savings account?
faq.a: Yes, significantly. Savings accounts offer 2.5–4% interest, while RDs offer 6.5–7.5%. If you have money sitting in a savings account for more than 1 month, moving it to an RD or FD earns substantially more interest.

howToSteps.name: Enter Monthly Deposit
howToSteps.text: Enter the fixed amount you will deposit every month into the RD.

howToSteps.name: Set Interest Rate
howToSteps.text: Enter your bank's RD interest rate. Indian bank RDs use quarterly compounding.

howToSteps.name: Choose Tenure
howToSteps.text: Enter the RD tenure in months. Minimum is 6 months.

howToSteps.name: View Maturity Value
howToSteps.text: The calculator shows total deposited, interest earned, and the final maturity amount.

schema.appDescription: Calculate recurring deposit maturity value and interest earned with quarterly compounding for Indian bank RDs
